serving.natural_language_portion: One standard chocolate crumble muffin weighing approximately 120 g, as typically sold at Singaporean café and bakery outlets.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Muffin, chocolate (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods reference)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central chocolate muffin reference entries (SR Legacy). The streusel topping increases fat and sugar slightly relative to a plain chocolate muffin; values from the input record (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) are consistent with USDA chocolate muffin ranges and have been retained with cross-check against USDA data. Input values accepted as plausible; confidence set to medium.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a chocolate crumble muffin. GI of 65 is estimated from the closest analogous food: 'muffin, blueberry' (GI ~59) and 'muffin, chocolate' analogues reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. The high sugar content (25 g/100 g) and refined wheat flour base support a medium-to-high GI estimate; 65 is used as a conservative medium-GI estimate. Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 50.0) / 100 = 32.5 (carbohydrate per 120 g serving = 41.67 × 1.2 = 50.0 g).

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (25 g/100 g) and elevated saturated fat (8.33 g/100 g); sodium at 250 mg/100 g is moderate but additive
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this muffin: sugar at 25 g/100 g independently elevates blood pressure risk (AHA 2018), saturated fat at 8.33 g/100 g contributes to arterial stiffness, and sodium at 250 mg/100 g adds further cardiovascular load; a 120 g serving delivers 300 mg sodium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar 25 g/100 g exceeds the 20 g/100 g glycaemic ceiling; estimated GI 65 with glycemic load 32.5 per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: This muffin is not suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes: sugar at 25 g/100 g is a hard disqualifier, the estimated GI of 65 combined with a glycemic load of 32.5 per 120 g serving will cause a large postprandial glucose spike, and the low dietary fibre (2.08 g/100 g) from refined wheat flour provides minimal glycaemic buffering.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 8.33 g/100 g, trans fat 0.25 g/100 g, cholesterol 41.67 mg/100 g, and high sugar 25 g/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this muffin: saturated fat at 8.33 g/100 g (from butter and chocolate chips) exceeds the 5 g/100 g CVD threshold, trans fat at 0.25 g/100 g raises LDL and lowers HDL, and high sugar at 25 g/100 g promotes dyslipidaemia and endothelial inflammation; a 120 g serving delivers approximately 10 g saturated fat.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat 8.33 g/100 g from butter (excluded from MUFA/PUFA carve-out) and pro-inflammatory sugar 25 g/100 g
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This muffin is not suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis: butter-derived saturated fat at 8.33 g/100 g exceeds the 5 g/100 g RA threshold and does not qualify for the MUFA/PUFA carve-out, and sugar at 25 g/100 g promotes TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokine activity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Chocolate Crumble Muffin is a Western-style baked good sold at Singaporean cafés and bakeries, consisting of a rich, moist chocolate muffin base topped with a sweet, buttery streusel crumble. The muffin base is made from refined wheat flour, cocoa powder, chocolate chips, butter, sugar, eggs, and milk, leavened with baking powder. The streusel topping adds a contrasting crunchy texture of flour, butter, and sugar baked to a golden crumble. The result is a dense, tender crumb with an intensely chocolatey flavour and a sweet, crisp topping. It is commonly consumed as a morning snack, afternoon tea item, or coffee accompaniment. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense at 375 kcal/100 g, with high fat (20.83 g/100 g, of which 8.33 g is saturated), high sugar (25 g/100 g), and moderate protein (5.42 g/100 g). The glycemic load per standard 120 g serving is approximately 32.5, making it clinically unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. It is vegetarian but contains gluten, dairy, and eggs. Allergen caution is warranted for soy (possible in chocolate chips).
common_user_queries: Is chocolate muffin good for diabetics?
Calories in a chocolate crumble muffin
Can I eat muffin with high blood pressure?

No exact approved-source match for this specific product exists; values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy chocolate muffin data. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008. Confidence set to medium.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this specific chocolate crumble muffin formulation is not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2024. potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this specific streusel-topped chocolate muffin formulation are not individually reported in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy muffin entries at the level of detail required; follow-up with HPB Singapore or laboratory analysis recommended. trans_fat_g of 0.25 g/100 g is retained from input; this is plausible for a butter-containing baked good. calcium_mg of 41.67 mg/100 g, iron_mg of 1.25 mg/100 g, and cholesterol_mg of 41.67 mg/100 g are retained from input and are consistent with USDA chocolate muffin reference values. Halal status is not confirmed; the input record does not assert halal certification.
